locator.value: Verified-video metadata reconstruction and response chronology
Source-family inventory and synchronized qualitative reconstruction
Frame-by-frame witness-view analysis of vehicle and agent topology
Agent-phone clip provenance, movement, commands, and unresolved contact
Video, dispatch, and fire-record synchronization of response actions
One-way road-pair context for Portland Avenue
2025-complete East 34th Street walking and biking improvements

assetTransformations.method: Map source-bounded qualitative street relationships into a non-georeferenced display frame while preserving attributed federal assertions and unresolved questions on separate layers.

claimRevisions.text: Renee Good was fatally shot in her vehicle by ICE agent Jonathan Ross in Minneapolis on January 7, 2026.
Official public records place the incident near East 34th Street and Portland Avenue in Minneapolis.
ABC's synchronized public-video analysis places the first gunshot at approximately 9:37:13 a.m. CST and three audible reports within about 0.7 seconds.
Public video analyses show Good's SUV stopped diagonally across part of Portland Avenue while other vehicles passed.
Public recordings contain repeated directions from an approaching agent for Good to get out while another agent reached toward the driver's door area.
Public video analyses show the SUV reverse briefly, then move forward while the steering wheel turned right; video alone does not establish the driver's intent.
DHS alleged that Good weaponized her vehicle, tried to kill officers, committed domestic terrorism, and was shot in self-defense.
The public footage does not show the vehicle passing over Ross and does not conclusively resolve whether brief physical contact occurred.
Independent visual analyses place Ross near the SUV's front-left area at the first shot and increasingly alongside the vehicle as firing continued.
Three gunshots are audible in under one second in the published recordings; the exact bullet paths and wound correlations are not established by public video.
The Medical Examiner's public data classifies Good's death as homicide caused by multiple gunshot wounds; homicide is a medical manner-of-death category, not a finding of murder or unlawful force.
Synchronized reporting indicates federal agents briefly assessed Good but did not visibly perform CPR before municipal responders arrived roughly six minutes after the shots.
The public record does not establish whether an earlier or different medical response would have changed the outcome.
Public reporting says the Justice Department did not open a federal criminal civil-rights investigation into Good's death.
HCAO and BCA are conducting a state investigation and say they have not prejudged whether any law was violated.

State and federal authorities initially contemplated joint work, but federal authorities ended evidence sharing on January 7 and state investigators reported being unable to complete a full use-of-force investigation.
HCAO submitted formal demands for physical evidence, original imagery, communications, statements, medical material, policy, training, and chain-of-custody records.
Minnesota, HCAO, and BCA filed a federal action seeking access to evidence withheld by DOJ and DHS.
On July 13, state authorities announced receipt of previously withheld hard drives and Good's vehicle; HCAO said analysis began immediately and remained ongoing.
No state charge, state declination, final independent use-of-force determination, or public final DHS report was identified by the July 17 research cutoff.

Public reporting does not establish whether Good-specific ICE body-camera footage exists; transferred hard drives reportedly include body-camera material from the broader Minnesota cases, but the wearer and incident are not identified.
The public visual record contains several camera families, but public copies are re-encoded and lack complete native metadata, authenticated clocks, lens calibration, and chain of custody.
The present scene is a source-linked street schematic, not metric photogrammetry, collision reconstruction, ballistics, or a trajectory analysis.
DHS use-of-force policy supplies an evaluative standard for deadly force and moving vehicles, but the policy alone does not establish compliance or violation in this incident.

claims.proceduralStatus: The death and identity of the shooting agent are established across official state/local records and independent reporting; legal justification is a separate unresolved question.
Intersection-level public location; no house address or tighter private-person location is retained.
unresolvedQuestions: What surveyed as-built control corresponds to the January 2026 street configuration?

claims.proceduralStatus: Publisher-derived media timing; high value for sequence but not an authenticated evidence-clock finding.
unresolvedQuestions: Do original files and device clocks confirm the absolute second and inter-shot intervals?

claims.proceduralStatus: Qualitative spatial observation from several published views; not a surveyed vehicle pose.
unresolvedQuestions: What exact vehicle footprint and curb coordinates are recoverable from original files and survey data?

claims.proceduralStatus: Audible/visible public-video sequence; legal authority, comprehension, and the participants' perceptions are not resolved by the recording alone.
unresolvedQuestions: What do original audio, agent statements, and operational records establish about commands and perception?

claims.proceduralStatus: Movement is source-bounded; labels such as attack, escape, or accident require additional evidence and legal analysis.
unresolvedQuestions: What do vehicle telemetry, steering data, and original calibrated views establish?

claims.proceduralStatus: Contemporaneous federal party assertion issued about two hours after the shooting; not an adjudicated or independent investigative finding.
unresolvedQuestions: What evidence and review process supported each characterization when it was published?

claims.proceduralStatus: Contact remains an open physical-evidence question; remaining upright does not exclude injury or brief contact.
unresolvedQuestions: What do original frames, vehicle inspection, clothing, medical records, and scene measurements establish about contact?

claims.proceduralStatus: Coarse relative topology from compressed public video; not a centimeter-level pose or trajectory finding.
unresolvedQuestions: Can independent originals and surveyed control produce validated pose estimates and uncertainty ellipses?

claims.proceduralStatus: Audio count and interval are publisher-supported; ballistic sequence and trajectory require physical evidence and qualified analysis.
unresolvedQuestions: What do casings, firearm examination, vehicle damage, and complete medical evidence establish?

claims.proceduralStatus: Public medical classification with an explicit legal-meaning boundary; graphic details and the full autopsy are excluded.
unresolvedQuestions: What non-graphic findings may lawfully be used by qualified reviewers if charging analysis requires them?

claims.proceduralStatus: Source-bounded response sequence; credentials, clinical assessments, and exact CPR start remain partly unresolved.
unresolvedQuestions: What do original video, responder records, and federal medical-response logs establish about assessments and treatment?

claims.proceduralStatus: Counterfactual clinical causation is outside what the public response timeline can prove.
unresolvedQuestions: Would qualified medical review of complete records support any counterfactual conclusion?

claims.proceduralStatus: Federal procedural decision as publicly reported; not an adjudication that the shooting was lawful.
unresolvedQuestions: Will DOJ publish a formal closing or declination memorandum?

claims.proceduralStatus: Active uncharged state investigation; investigative offenses and evidence requests are not charges or probable-cause findings.
unresolvedQuestions: When will HCAO publish a charging or declination decision and supporting rationale?

claims.proceduralStatus: State institutional account of investigative coordination; separate from the merits of the shooting.
unresolvedQuestions: What federal records describe the change in coordination and its legal basis?

claims.proceduralStatus: Evidence request establishes investigatory scope, not that every requested item exists or supports a violation.
unresolvedQuestions: Which requested categories were produced, withheld, unavailable, or never created?

claims.proceduralStatus: Filed civil evidence-access litigation; allegations in pleadings are not merits findings about the shooting.
unresolvedQuestions: How will the July evidence exchange affect the still-pending action?

claims.proceduralStatus: Most current official evidence posture; the announcement does not itemize every file or prove production is complete.
unresolvedQuestions: What evidence inventory and chain-of-custody documentation will be publicly described after review?

claims.proceduralStatus: Current procedural gap. Absence from reviewed public pages is not proof that no nonpublic decision or internal record exists.
unresolvedQuestions: Obtain any future charging, declination, or final administrative-review record from its official source.

claims.proceduralStatus: Do not mislabel Ross's handheld cellphone video as bodycam footage.
unresolvedQuestions: Which body-camera files, wearers, clocks, and incidents are represented in the transferred material?

claims.proceduralStatus: Media-provenance limitation; analyses by different publishers may use the same underlying recordings.
unresolvedQuestions: Can lawful originals be obtained with device metadata and creator attestations?

claims.proceduralStatus: Forensic-method conclusion for this private first pass.
unresolvedQuestions: Can original independent files, surveyed as-built control, vehicle scans, and physical evidence support a validated reconstruction?

claims.proceduralStatus: Policy context only; legal and factual application remains under investigation.
unresolvedQuestions: What operative training, briefings, and officer-perception evidence apply to the encounter?

confidenceAssessments.method: Editorial descriptor based on source status, independence, precision, and access to underlying evidence; bounds are review aids, not statistical probabilities.

uncertainty: Which body-camera files, wearers, clocks, and incidents are represented in the transferred material?

confidenceAssessments.method: Editorial descriptor based on source status, independence, precision, and access to underlying evidence; bounds are review aids, not statistical probabilities.
confidenceAssessments.rationale: Media-provenance limitation; analyses by different publishers may use the same underlying recordings.
uncertainty: Can lawful originals be obtained with device metadata and creator attestations?

confidenceAssessments.method: Editorial descriptor based on source status, independence, precision, and access to underlying evidence; bounds are review aids, not statistical probabilities.
confidenceAssessments.rationale: Forensic-method conclusion for this private first pass.
uncertainty: Can original independent files, surveyed as-built control, vehicle scans, and physical evidence support a validated reconstruction?

confidenceAssessments.method: Editorial descriptor based on source status, independence, precision, and access to underlying evidence; bounds are review aids, not statistical probabilities.
confidenceAssessments.rationale: Policy context only; legal and factual application remains under investigation.
uncertainty: What operative training, briefings, and officer-perception evidence apply to the encounter?

alternateExplanations: Different operational and ordinary-language meanings of block
Rhetorical compression in the initial account
contradictions.description: Video analyses show partial diagonal obstruction while other vehicles passed; DHS characterized the vehicle as having been used to block the road. Both source expressions remain visible.
contradictions.title: Road obstruction terminology

contradictions.description: Observed reverse/forward/right movement does not by itself establish the DHS allegations of deliberate weaponization or attempted killing.
contradictions.title: Vehicle intent and weaponization

alternateExplanations: Contact not visible in compressed views
contradictions.description: The federal account invoked danger to officers; public views do not show a run-over and do not conclusively resolve brief contact or injury.
contradictions.title: Physical contact with agent

alternateExplanations: Different reference points for front versus side
contradictions.description: Independent analyses place Ross near the front-left and increasingly alongside; the public record cannot recover a complete body pose or legal perception from that topology alone.
contradictions.title: Agent position during firing

contradictions.description: DOJ reportedly did not open a federal civil-rights investigation while the state investigation continued after federal evidence access was curtailed.
contradictions.title: Federal and state investigative posture

alternateExplanations: Remaining dispute over inventory or access terms
contradictions.description: The state moved from curtailed access, to formal demands and litigation, to receipt of hard drives and the vehicle. The July transfer does not prove every requested item was produced.
contradictions.title: Evidence access over time

alternateExplanations: Clock offset between records and recordings
contradictions.description: Public synchronizations broadly align on delayed visible municipal resuscitation but vary on exact CPR-start labeling; outcome causation remains unproved.
contradictions.title: Medical response timing and treatment

alternateExplanations: Medical and legal classifications answer different questions
contradictions.description: The Medical Examiner's homicide classification records death caused by another person; it does not answer murder, criminal liability, or justification.
contradictions.title: Medical homicide versus legal culpability

findings: Twenty-four exact public locators are registered with retrieval metadata.
Current official posture is separated from contemporaneous agency claims and publisher analysis.
limitations: No remote source body, paid docket record, or authenticated account content is retained.
Search-negative procedural statements may require future refresh.

findings: Copyrighted video and reporting are metadata/link-only.
The 3D scene contains project-authored geometry and no source pixels, graphic imagery, faces, plates, or private addresses.
limitations: Public release requires item-specific qualified rights, privacy, legal, and editorial review.

findings: Scene is qualitative, relational, non-georeferenced, and source-linked.
Attributed federal claims and unresolved questions occupy distinct layers.
No trajectory, collision, intent, identity, or clinical-causation solve is claimed.
limitations: No rights-cleared native multi-view set, authenticated master clock, or complete chain of custody was available.
Road widths, vehicle dimensions, display orientation, paths, camera positions, and all distances are illustrative.
The display separates observed motion from the DHS account; neither layer establishes intent or legal justification.
No bullet path, wound path, collision solve, vehicle speed, physical-contact determination, or clinical counterfactual is shown.
No faces, children, graphic injury, private addresses, plates, witness positions, or sensitive tactical detail are included.

findings: The shooting agent is identified as an uncharged subject of an open state investigation.
Homicide is labeled as a medical category, not murder.
Initial DHS claims remain attributed and are not presented as findings.
limitations: Future charging, declination, court, or administrative records may change the posture.

findings: No crawler, outreach, authentication bypass, private-person research, deployment, or publication was performed.
No graphic media or sensitive location detail was ingested.
limitations: A production private workspace still requires authentication, authorization, encryption, audit, retention, and egress controls.

entities.canonicalName: East 34th Street and Portland Avenue incident area
State of Minnesota v. U.S. Department of Justice

events.description: Publisher synchronization shows Good's SUV partly across Portland Avenue while traffic could pass.
events.title: Vehicle visible in diagonal position

events.description: Recordings show agents exit a federal vehicle and approach the driver's side with repeated directions to exit.
events.title: Federal agents approach

events.description: Publisher analysis places three reports in under one second beginning at approximately 9:37:13 a.m.; trajectory and legal justification remain unresolved.
events.title: Fatal shooting

events.description: The SUV continues and crashes nearby within seconds of the gunfire.
events.title: Vehicle reaches nearby final-rest region

events.description: Synchronized reporting records a bystander identifying as a physician offering help.
events.title: Bystander physician offers assistance

events.description: Public synchronization places the first municipal/fire responder at the vehicle roughly six minutes after the shots.
events.title: Municipal responders arrive

events.description: Published synchronization shows municipal lifesaving efforts; exact CPR-start terminology differs.
events.title: Municipal resuscitation sequence

events.description: DHS publishes claims of vehicle weaponization, attempted killing, domestic terrorism, and self-defense; these remain attributed.
events.title: Initial DHS account

events.description: Public ME data records multiple gunshot wounds and manner of death as homicide; this is not a legal culpability finding.
events.title: Medical Examiner classification

events.description: HCAO submits Touhy demands for federal evidence needed by the state investigation.
events.title: Formal state evidence demands

events.description: Minnesota, HCAO, and BCA file a federal action seeking access to evidence.
events.title: Evidence-access lawsuit filed

events.description: State authorities announce receipt of hard drives and Good's vehicle and begin analysis without prejudgment.
events.title: Federal evidence and vehicle transferred

events.description: No public state charge, declination, final independent use-of-force determination, or final DHS report was identified.
events.title: Research cutoff

glossary.definition: Supported within the package by the strongest available source state; not a universal or judicial finding.
A proposition presented as a named source's claim rather than adopted as fact.
The source link and descriptive record are retained without the remote response body.
A relational visual model that is not metric, geodetic, ballistic, or photogrammetric.

warnings: Internal consistency does not authenticate remote source content.
Human and tool actor identities are not cryptographically authenticated.

investigations.purpose: Build a provenance-first, non-graphic first-pass reconstruction of the January 7, 2026 fatal federal-agent shooting of Renee Good while preserving disputed intent, current uncharged investigative posture, source conflicts, rights limits, response chronology, and spatial uncertainty.
scope: Contradictions, corrections, rights, dignity, privacy, and evidence gaps
investigations.title: Renee Good killing - public-record reconstruction

methodology.exclusionRule: No crawler execution, authenticated access, raw third-party body retention, publication, or unsupported identity, guilt, trajectory, or metric inference.
methodology.inclusionRule: Public records and specifically reviewed metadata-only fixtures relevant to the defined investigation scope.
scope: Contradictions, corrections, rights, dignity, privacy, and evidence gaps

observations.description: Published video analyses show an SUV diagonally occupying part of Portland Avenue while other vehicles passed.

locators.value: 9:35:05 sequence

observations.description: Published recordings show federal agents approach the driver's side and contain repeated directions to exit.

locators.value: 9:37:08 sequence

observations.description: Synchronized analyses show a brief reverse followed by forward/right movement before the shooting.

locators.value: Qualitative synchronized movement

observations.description: Three reports occur in under one second in publisher analysis; no public-media trajectory solve is available.

locators.value: Inter-shot timing

observations.description: Published response synchronization shows a brief federal assessment, help offers, and later municipal response.

locators.value: Response synchronization

observations.description: The January 2026 street form includes recent East 34th walking and biking changes that older basemaps may not show.

locators.value: Completed project context

spatialAnchors.label: Incident intersection region
spatialAnchors.method: Intersection-level placement from official records; no house address or geodetic coordinate retained.

spatialAnchors.label: Initial diagonal SUV region
spatialAnchors.method: Qualitative publisher-verified video topology; not a surveyed pose.

spatialAnchors.label: Agent approach region
spatialAnchors.method: Coarse relative placement from synchronized public views.

spatialAnchors.label: Ross first-shot region
spatialAnchors.method: Front-left qualitative topology; not a body-pose or trajectory solution.

spatialAnchors.label: Vehicle motion corridor
spatialAnchors.method: Display-only reverse-then-forward/right topology; distances and curvature are not measurements.

spatialAnchors.label: Vehicle final-rest region
spatialAnchors.method: Nearby post-shot crash region; no address, exact obstacle, or surveyed pose retained.

spatialAnchors.label: Witness-camera family A
spatialAnchors.method: Illustrative camera zone; exact witness position, orientation, lens, and identity withheld or unknown.

spatialAnchors.label: Witness-camera family B
spatialAnchors.method: Illustrative camera zone; exact witness position, orientation, lens, and identity withheld or unknown.

spatialAnchors.label: Federal-agent handheld camera
spatialAnchors.method: Attached to a coarse agent region only; it is a cellphone source, not authenticated body-camera footage.

spatialAnchors.label: Municipal medical-response region
spatialAnchors.method: Response zone synchronized to public recordings and public records; not a clinical treatment reconstruction.
